title: Floral Silky Argentinian Wild Garlic Leaves Stir-fry
description: A floral and silky brunch-friendly dish featuring wild garlic leaves, seasoned with a touch of curing for added depth and flavor. Suitable for egg-free diets.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 200°F (93°C). Rinse the wild garlic leaves and pat them dry with a clean kitchen towel.
Slice the cured ham thinly. Place the sliced ham in a baking dish and bake it for about 15 minutes until it's warm and slightly crispy, but not burnt.
In a wok or large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add finely chopped onion and minced garlic, sautéing for about 2-3 minutes until the onion turns translucent and fragrant.
Add the wild garlic leaves to the skillet, tossing them in with the onions and garlic for about 1-2 more minutes. Season with salt if needed.
Garnish with fresh parsley leaves before serving.
